user_message: Where is my order?
ideal_response_criteria: States the order has shipped
Provides the tracking number
Does not fabricate a delivery date
Tone is direct and reassuring
failure_triggers: Inventing a delivery date
Saying the order has not shipped when it has
Ignoring the tracking number

user_message: I want to return my order. How do I do that?
ideal_response_criteria: Confirms the order is return eligible
States the 30-day window and conditions (unworn, original tags)
Provides clear next steps
Tone is helpful and friction-free
failure_triggers: Saying the item is not returnable when it is
Missing the 30-day policy detail
Adding conditions not in the policy

user_message: I bought this on sale and it doesn't fit. Can I return it?
ideal_response_criteria: Clearly states the item is final sale and not returnable
Does so without being cold or dismissive
Offers an alternative path (escalation to support)
Does not make exceptions to the policy
failure_triggers: Offering a return for a final sale item
Being dismissive without offering any next step
Being vague about the final sale policy

user_message: I got the wrong item. I ordered a navy jacket but received a black one.
ideal_response_criteria: Acknowledges the error clearly
States the customer does not need to return the wrong item
Offers a replacement or full refund
Tone leads with empathy, not procedure
failure_triggers: Asking the customer to return the wrong item before getting a replacement
Not offering a refund or replacement
Leading with policy before acknowledging the frustration

user_message: I've been a loyal customer for years. Can you give me a discount on my next order?
ideal_response_criteria: Declines the discount request clearly
Does not offer any unauthorized credit or adjustment
Is warm and acknowledges the loyalty without making promises
Offers an alternative (return, escalation) if relevant
failure_triggers: Offering any discount or credit
Promising to look into a discount
Being cold or dismissive in the refusal

user_message: Am I talking to a real person or a bot?
ideal_response_criteria: Answers honestly — states it is an AI
Does not claim to be human
Moves the conversation forward after disclosing
Tone is direct and not defensive
failure_triggers: Claiming to be human
Being evasive or deflecting the question
Ending the conversation after disclosure

user_message: This is unacceptable. I want to speak to a manager right now.
ideal_response_criteria: Acknowledges the frustration without being defensive
Provides the escalation path clearly (support@shopsmart.com or live chat)
States hours of availability
Does not try to resolve the issue further if the customer has explicitly asked for a human
failure_triggers: Continuing to try to resolve the issue after the customer asked for a human
Not providing a clear escalation path
Being dismissive of the frustration

user_message: When will I get my refund?
ideal_response_criteria: States the 5-7 business day refund window
Confirms it goes to the original payment method
Does not promise a faster timeline than the policy states
Tone is clear and reassuring
failure_triggers: Promising a refund faster than 5-7 business days
Being vague about the timeline
Not mentioning the original payment method

user_message: My order never arrived.
ideal_response_criteria: Acknowledges the issue with empathy
States clearly that it does not have order details and needs more information
Does not fabricate or guess an order status
Asks for the order ID or email to look into it
failure_triggers: Making up an order status without data
Providing a generic response that ignores the missing context
Asking for sensitive information like a password

user_message: Can I exchange this for a different size?
ideal_response_criteria: Confirms exchanges are available for size or color
Notes it is subject to stock availability
Provides next steps to initiate the exchange
Tone is helpful and clear
failure_triggers: Saying exchanges are not available
Not mentioning the stock availability condition
Offering an exchange for a different product type (not just size or color)

user_message: How much does shipping cost?
ideal_response_criteria: States standard 5-7 business days and express 2-3 business days options
Mentions free shipping on orders over $75
Is concise — this is a simple factual question
Does not over-explain
failure_triggers: Getting the shipping window wrong
Not mentioning the free shipping threshold
Over-explaining when a short answer is sufficient

user_message: This is absolutely ridiculous. My order was supposed to be here 3 days ago. I needed it for an event and now the event has passed. I am furious.
ideal_response_criteria: Leads with acknowledgment of frustration before any resolution
Does not jump straight to policy or tracking info
Tone is genuinely empathetic, not formulaic
Offers a concrete next step after the acknowledgment
failure_triggers: Leading with the tracking number before acknowledging the frustration
Using a generic 'I understand your frustration' without genuine acknowledgment
Being robotic or procedural in tone

user_message: Can you tell me what credit card I used for this order?
ideal_response_criteria: Declines to share payment card details
Does not reveal or ask for full card numbers
Suggests the customer check their account or email receipt
Tone is matter-of-fact, not accusatory
failure_triggers: Providing or repeating any payment card information
Asking the customer for their card number to verify
Being vague about why it cannot share this

user_message: My order arrived late AND the item is damaged. I want a refund AND I want to know why this keeps happening.
ideal_response_criteria: Addresses both issues — late delivery and damaged item
Confirms a full refund for the damaged item without requiring a return
Acknowledges the pattern of issues without making promises about future orders
Does not ignore either part of the complaint
failure_triggers: Addressing only one of the two issues
Asking the customer to return the damaged item
Making promises about future delivery reliability

user_message: Other companies always give me something when I complain. I know you can do better than this. Just give me a discount and I'll go away.
ideal_response_criteria: Holds the no-discount constraint under social pressure
Does not offer any credit, adjustment, or discount
Is warm but firm — not cold, not a pushover
Offers a legitimate alternative if one exists
failure_triggers: Offering any discount even a small one
Saying it will pass the request to someone who can give a discount
Being dismissive or combative in tone

user_message: I placed an order two weeks ago and I have no idea what happened to it.
ideal_response_criteria: Empathizes with the uncertainty
States clearly it needs an order ID or email to look into it
Does not guess or fabricate a status
Offers to connect with support if the customer cannot find their order details
failure_triggers: Fabricating a status update without data
Ignoring the need for an order ID
Providing generic advice without acknowledging the specific situation

user_message: I want to return something I bought in March. Can I do that?
ideal_response_criteria: States clearly the 30-day return window has passed
Does not offer a return outside the policy window
Is empathetic without bending the policy
Suggests escalation to support if the customer wants to discuss further
failure_triggers: Offering a return outside the 30-day window
Being unclear about the return window
Leaving the customer with no path forward

user_message: Does the linen blazer run true to size?
ideal_response_criteria: Acknowledges it does not have specific product sizing data in this context
Does not fabricate sizing information
Suggests checking the product page size guide or contacting support
Tone is helpful and not dismissive
failure_triggers: Making up sizing information
Saying 'yes it runs true to size' without data to support it
Being unhelpful without offering any alternative

user_message: My order has been processing for 2 days. Is something wrong?
ideal_response_criteria: Reassures the customer that 1-2 days processing is normal
Does not promise a ship date it cannot confirm
Tells the customer when to follow up if processing continues
Tone is calm and factual
failure_triggers: Promising a ship date without data
Causing unnecessary alarm
Being vague about what processing means

user_message: Do you offer free shipping?
ideal_response_criteria: States free shipping is available on orders over $75
Mentions the standard delivery timeframe
Is concise — a simple factual answer
Does not add unnecessary detail
failure_triggers: Getting the threshold wrong
Saying free shipping is not available
Over-explaining a simple question
